topic: How to Configure Every AI Coding Assistant 2026: CLAUDE.md, Cursor Rules, Copilot

competitors.title: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and Every AI Config File Explained
key_points: Complete landscape of 9 config file formats: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, .cursorrules, .cursor/rules/*.mdc, copilot-instructions.md, .github/instructions/*.instructions.md, .windsurfrules, .windsurf/rules/*.md
CLAUDE.md has 3-location hierarchy: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (global), ./CLAUDE.md (project root), subdirectory CLAUDE.md files (scoped)
AGENTS.md is closest to universal standard, stewarded by Linux Foundation with 60,000+ open-source projects adoption
Cursor evolved from single .cursorrules to .cursor/rules/*.mdc with 4 activation modes: Always On, Auto Attached, Model Decision, Manual
GitHub Copilot supports scoped instructions via .github/instructions/*.instructions.md with glob frontmatter since July 2025, org-level instructions GA April 2026
Key mistake: writing config files over 500 lines — LLMs reliably follow only ~150-200 instructions; Claude Code system prompt uses ~50 slots already
Best practice: start with AGENTS.md as single source of truth, add tool-specific files only when needed features AGENTS.md can't provide
Use linters instead of instructions for code style: 'never send an LLM to do a linter's job'

competitors.title: AI Coding Assistant Config Files Compared: CLAUDE.md vs .cursorrules vs copilot-instructions.md
key_points: Config fragmentation problem: 5 projects × 3 AI tools = 15 config files for developers who take AI-assisted development seriously
CLAUDE.md has 5-layer hierarchy (most sophisticated), .cursorrules is single file (simplest), copilot-instructions.md has 2 layers
Only CLAUDE.md has token visibility and memory/learning features across all formats compared

Comparison matrix: CLAUDE.md (5 layers, global config, token visibility, memory/learning), Cursor (single file), Copilot (2 layers, org-level), Windsurf (single file, 6K/12K char limits), AGENTS.md (single file, 60K+ projects)
Token budget recommendation: aim for 100-200 lines per config file, use official tokenizers to check token counts
Future prediction: either a standard emerges (like .editorconfig) or tools start reading each other's files
The content you put in config files is more important than which format you use

competitors.title: Claude Code vs Cursor vs Copilot: How AI Assistants Handle Rules
key_points: Five-dimension comparison: file format, scope, hierarchy, content capabilities, and ecosystem
Claude Code processes full markdown including code blocks, tables, and conditional rules; Cursor uses plain text instructions; Copilot is most constrained with concise statements
Files don't conflict: CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, and .github/copilot-instructions.md can coexist in same repo
Hierarchy ranking: Claude Code (root + subdirectory) > Cursor (single file) = Copilot (single file)
Content richness ranking: Claude Code (full markdown + conditionals) > Cursor (markdown basics) > Copilot (concise statements)
IDE integration ranking: Copilot (VS Code native) = Cursor (dedicated IDE) > Claude Code (CLI + IDE extensions)
Practical approach: maintain a canonical rule source, generate format-specific files from it; core rules are universal across tools

competitors.title: CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md: The Configuration Layer That Makes AI Coding Agents Actually Follow Your Rules
key_points: Instruction budget problem: frontier LLMs follow ~150-200 instructions; Claude Code system prompt consumes ~50 slots; recommended ceiling is ~300 lines with @imports for more
CLAUDE.md unique features: @import syntax for modularization, path-scoped rules via YAML frontmatter with globs, 5-level hierarchy, auto-memory layer, hooks for deterministic behaviors
AGENTS.md is open standard under Linux Foundation, supported by 60+ tools (Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, Aider, Gemini CLI, Zed, Warp, Devin, JetBrains Junie)
Codex implementation: 3-tier hierarchy (global ~/.codex/AGENTS.md → project root → subdirectory AGENTS.override.md), 32 KiB byte limit, configurable fallback filenames
OpenAI's codex repository itself uses 88 AGENTS.md files distributed across its directory structure
Practical approach: AGENTS.md holds universal rules for any agent, CLAUDE.md imports AGENTS.md and adds Claude-specific enhancements; copilot-instructions.md can be symlinked from AGENTS.md
Use a linter instead of instructions for code style: 'Never send an LLM to do a linter's job'
What to include: toolchain specifics, workflow expectations, non-obvious constraints, architectural decisions; what to exclude: anything inferable from code, standard conventions, generic advice

stats.fact: 76-85% of developers adopted AI coding assistants in 2026, with 50% daily usage
GitHub Copilot leads adoption at 48%, followed by Cursor at 25%
70% of engineers use 2-4 AI tools simultaneously, with 15% using 5 or more
stats.source: Exceeds AI citing Pragmatic Engineer Survey 2026

stats.fact: Frontier LLMs can reliably follow ~150-200 instructions; Claude Code system prompt uses ~50 of those slots
AGENTS.md is supported by 60+ tools and 60,000+ open-source projects under Linux Foundation stewardship
OpenAI's codex repository uses 88 AGENTS.md files distributed across its directory structure
Code review time increases 91% with AI-generated code, bug rates increase 9%, while cycle time decreases 15%
Config fragmentation: 5 projects × 3 AI tools = 15 config files for developers using multiple AI assistants
GitHub Copilot scoped instructions (.github/instructions/*.instructions.md) with glob frontmatter available since July 2025, org-level instructions GA April 2026
Windsurf character limits: individual rule files capped at 6,000 characters, total combined rules must not exceed 12,000 characters
METR study found experienced developers were 19% slower with AI coding tools despite perceiving a 20% speedup
HumanLayer keeps their CLAUDE.md under 60 lines; reasonable ceiling for most projects is 300 lines
30% of developers use at least two AI coding assistants simultaneously
stats.source: Exceeds AI citing Cyberhaven 2026 AI Adoption Report

angles: Config fragmentation crisis: developers using multiple AI tools face maintaining 15+ config files across projects — AGENTS.md as universal standard solves this
Instruction budget constraint: LLMs can only follow ~150-200 instructions effectively, making ruthless prioritization more important than comprehensiveness
The linter-first principle: 'Never send an LLM to do a linter's job' — use deterministic tools for style, save AI config instructions for what only context can solve
AGENTS.md as single source of truth: start with universal format, add tool-specific files (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules) only when needed for advanced features
The multi-tool reality: 70% of engineers use 2-4 AI tools simultaneously, making cross-tool config management a real productivity problem
Evolution from simple to sophisticated: Cursor moved from single .cursorrules to .cursor/rules/*.mdc with 4 activation modes; GitHub Copilot added scoped instructions with glob patterns
Hook vs instruction distinction: for behaviors that must happen with zero exceptions (linter runs, test gates), use deterministic hooks rather than advisory instructions
keywords: CLAUDE.md vs cursorrules vs copilot instructions
